name: Paseo por el embalse de Valmayor — Sierra de Guadarrama
region_intro: Mountain biking around El Escorial offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, characterized by pine forests, granite formations, and significant elevation changes. The region features prominent peaks like Mount Abantos, providing challenging climbs and descents. Routes also explore the scenic Valmayor Reservoir and the accessible La Herrería Forest, offering a mix of landscapes for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around El Escorial The most popular mountain bike trail is Paseo por el embalse de Valmayor — Sierra de Guadarrama, a 21.8 miles (35.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers picturesque lakeside riding around the Valmayor Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Seat of Philip II – Abantos Summit Sign loop from El Escorial, a difficult 24.7 miles (39.8 km) path. This route features considerable elevation gain and scenic views from Mount Abantos. Local mountain bikers also love the Puerto del Malagón y Monasterio — Sierra de Guadarrama, a 12.8 miles (20.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant climbs, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Mountain biking around El Escorial is defined by the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, Valmayor Reservoir, and Abantos Pine Woods. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in El Escorial are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 160 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Escorial's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in El Escorial?
A: El Escorial boasts a diverse network of mountain biking trails. While komoot features 54 specific mountain bike tours, the region offers over 160 cycling routes in total, catering to various skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around El Escorial?
A: The terrain around El Escorial is highly varied, situated within the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains. You'll encounter pine forests, granite formations, and significant elevation changes, particularly around prominent peaks like Mount Abantos. Routes also explore scenic areas like the Valmayor Reservoir and the accessible La Herrería Forest, offering a mix of challenging climbs, exhilarating descents, and picturesque lakeside riding.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in El Escorial?
A: Yes, El Escorial offers routes suitable for beginners. While many trails feature moderate to difficult sections, there are options for less strenuous rides, particularly around the Valmayor Reservoir. For a moderate option with picturesque lakeside riding, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352217231/paseo-por-el-embalse-de-valmayor-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo por el embalse de Valmayor — Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I can visit while mountain biking in El Escorial?
A: El Escorial is rich in scenic viewpoints and natural attractions. A notable spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/740607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seat of Philip II&lt;/a&gt;, which offers breathtaking panoramic views of the Royal Monastery and the surrounding landscape. You can also explore areas around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2505177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Abantos&lt;/a&gt; for stunning vistas, or ride along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5155198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmayor Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; for picturesque lakeside scenery. For a natural highlight, look for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2877011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covacho Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in El Escorial?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in El Escorial are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example of a challenging circular route that offers significant elevation gain and scenic views from Mount Abantos is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40365732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seat of Philip II – Abantos Summit Sign loop from El Escorial&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in El Escorial?
A: The mountain bike trails in El Escorial are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have explored the area using komoot, often praising the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain climbs to serene lakeside paths, and the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails in El Escorial using public transport?
A: El Escorial is generally accessible by public transport, making it possible to reach trailheads without a car. Many routes start directly from the town of El Escorial, which has good connections. It's advisable to check local bus or train schedules to ensure they accommodate your bike, especially during peak times.

Q: Are there any trails with significant elevation gain for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. El Escorial, being in the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, offers numerous trails with considerable elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1336277020/puerto-del-malagon-y-monasterio-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Malagón y Monasterio — Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt; route features nearly 800 meters of ascent over its 20.5 km. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40365732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seat of Philip II – Abantos Summit Sign loop from El Escorial&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1000 meters of climbing.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in El Escorial?
A: The best time for mountain biking in El Escorial generally spans spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, with less extreme heat than summer and fewer chances of snow or heavy rain than winter. This allows for comfortable riding conditions and vibrant natural scenery.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in El Escorial?
A: While many trails in El Escorial can be challenging, there are options that families might find more suitable, especially those with less technical sections and moderate distances. Routes around the Valmayor Reservoir, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352217231/paseo-por-el-embalse-de-valmayor-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo por el embalse de Valmayor — Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt;, offer picturesque lakeside riding that can be enjoyed by families looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trails in El Escorial?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the town of El Escorial, as well as at various trailheads leading into the Sierra de Guadarrama. Specific parking areas may vary depending on your chosen starting point, but many routes are accessible from established parking lots or roadside pull-offs near popular natural areas like La Herrería Forest or the Valmayor Reservoir.

Q: Are there any specific trails that offer a good mix of technical sections and flowy paths?
A: El Escorial's diverse trail network includes routes that blend technical challenges with more flowy sections. Some trails feature technical elements like roots, sharp turns, and deep ruts, particularly in the mountainous areas, while others provide smoother, multi-use paths. For a challenging ride that combines varied terrain, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1332771176/el-escorial-valmayor-y-las-siete-cancelas-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Escorial, Valmayor y las Siete Cancelas — Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt; route.

Depending on the time of year, and if the reservoir is more or less full, we will have to get our feet wet. It is not a major problem, but it is advisable to bring spare socks

Church of the old village of Valmayor, it became a Hermitage in 1648, when the town was declared depopulated as a result of the plague of 1600. Built from a legendary apparition of the Virgin of Hope to some shepherds.
Recently rebuilt, the chancel and belfry are preserved, with a nave with a pointed arch and a main chapel with cross vaults. The interior altarpiece from the 17th century. XVII, represents a modern image of the Virgin, imitating the one that disappeared in 1936. This is taken to the town in May, to be returned in a pilgrimage in June.

It is the object of great devotion on the part of the beehives. It is located in a beautiful location on the road to the Valmayor swamp. Built in 1999, it is a simple construction, with beautiful windows, made of granite and slate roof. It has a viewpoint and on a rock there is a cruise that announces Mariana devotion with the stone image of Santiago Apostle.

Moving away from the Valmayor reservoir, this path parallel to the Cañada Real Segoviana appears presided over by the views of the Cuerda Larga.

The Church of San Bartolomé, better known as the Hermitage of El Cerrillo, was built in 1447. Its construction was financed through a donation of 1,000 maravedis from the Marquis of Santillana. Due to its poor condition in the late 17th and early 18th centuries, it was rebuilt with granite stone. The final necessary renovation was carried out in the 20th century. Currently, the Galapagar pilgrimage is celebrated on the second Sunday in May in honor of Our Lady of the Forsaken.
